raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the contribution claim and reframing the stated thesis around robustness and regularization.","R1.2":"The request entails checking whether the argument from method mechanism to robustness conclusions is coherent and internally consistent.","R1.3":"Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing because the artifact claims OOD robustness while reporting standard test-set top-1 accuracy, creating a central support gap.","R1.5":"Strengthening the passage may require reordering section-level emphasis so motivation, evidence, claims, and limitations form a clearer narrative arc.","R1.6":"The delegation naturally includes improving sentence-level force, concision, register, and flow.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is active because stronger research writing should distinguish demonstrated results from hypotheses or interpretations.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact makes broad claims about OOD robustness, fundamental ERM limitations, and safety-critical deployment.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is active to ensure the response stays within improving the draft rather than inventing experiments or unsupported results.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is active because the artifact invokes safety-critical domains where overstated robustness could mislead deployment expectations.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability is active because revisions should preserve which claims come from the provided artifact and avoid fabricating external support."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the contribution claim and reframing the stated thesis around robustness and regularization.
rationale.R1.2: The request entails checking whether the argument from method mechanism to robustness conclusions is coherent and internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing because the artifact claims OOD robustness while reporting standard test-set top-1 accuracy, creating a central support gap.
rationale.R1.5: Strengthening the passage may require reordering section-level emphasis so motivation, evidence, claims, and limitations form a clearer narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally includes improving sentence-level force, concision, register, and flow.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is active because stronger research writing should distinguish demonstrated results from hypotheses or interpretations.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact makes broad claims about OOD robustness, fundamental ERM limitations, and safety-critical deployment.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is active to ensure the response stays within improving the draft rather than inventing experiments or unsupported results.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because the artifact invokes safety-critical domains where overstated robustness could mislead deployment expectations.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is active because revisions should preserve which claims come from the provided artifact and avoid fabricating external support.
